Off Calendar Dec-02-2021 Continued To Feb-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-16-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randall Boessenecker, via ZOOM, for Shrita Williams, Gloria Williams, and Lillian Williams-Jackson, Petitioners; Matthew Gluck, via ZOOM, for Betty Williams, Objector.

Mr. Gluck reports that the parties have a settlement in principal and are working on the main issue which is the real property and will continue to work on settling the case. The court advises that it can order the matter to pro bono mediation if needed. Mr. Gluck advises that the matter may likely resolve this week or the next. Mr. Boessenecker proposes continuing the matter for a few weeks to allow the parties to work on settlement. There is discussion of the upcoming Motion for Summary Judgment with Mr. Gluck advising that if granted it would dispose of opposing counsel's petition, but that one additional issue would remain. The court advises the parties that it is ordering the matter to pro bono mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. The parties are to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order. Mediation must be completed no later than Jan-24-2022. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Jan-28-2022. The Parties are to return on Feb-08-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

The Case Management Hearing is off calendar. The remaining matters are continued to Feb-08-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-30-2021 Continued To Dec-10-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Betty Williams Petition For Partition
Off Calendar Aug-31-2021 Continued To Nov-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew Gluck, via ZOOM, for Betty Williams, Objector; John Boessenecker, via ZOOM, for Shrita Williams, Gloria Williams and Lillian Williams Jackson, Petitioners.

The court begins by acknowledging receipt of the parties' joint status statement. The court then confirm with Mr. Boessenecker that Mr. Williams died in San Francisco. The court then queries Mr. Gluck regarding the delivery of courtesy copies of his petition, proposed order thereon and proper notice of hearing on the mandatory judicial council form. Mr. Gluck reports that all had been delivered to the court one week ago. The court advises Mr. Gluck that it appears that the court did not receive the copies and instructs Mr. Gluck to follow-up with the assigned Examiner regarding courtesy copies of his petition, the proposed order and notice of hearing by the next calling of this matter or the court will consider taking his petition off calendar. The parties are then advised that the court will likely order the parties to participate in some form of Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) and queries counsel as to their current positions regarding the preceding. Mr. Bossenecker reports that he is ready to participate in settlement discussions but that it might be best to defer settlement discussions until after the real property is sold. Mr. Gluck agrees and points to the fact that the court's decision on a pending motion for summary judgment will help with settlement discussions as well. The parties are advised to file a joint case management statement prior to the next calling of this matter, pursuant to the court's previous order setting case management hearings.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Partition and Second Amended Petition for Transfer, etc., are continued to 11/16/2021. The Case Management Hearing is off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker, via ZOOM, counsel for Shrita Williams; Gloria Williams, and Lillian Williams Jackson, Petitioners; Matthew Gluck, via ZOOM, counsel for Betty Williams, Respondent.

At the onset, the court advises Mr. Gluck he has yet to file proper notice regarding Betty Williams' petition and, reminds Mr. Gluck that he has not yet delivered a courtesy copy of said petition an proposed order thereon, to the Examiner in room 202. After additional discussion, the court advises counsel that the court will be issuing a Case Management order, setting the following hearing dates for Case Management: August 10, 2021; November 16, 2021. The court also orders that trial in this matter is set for February 8, 2022. All preceding hearing dates are set for 9:00 am in Department 204. Mr. Boessenecker is ordered to monitor the court's register of actions and to serve all parties with the Case Management order once it is available to print.

The Second Amended Petition for Transfer, etc., and Betty Williams' Petition for Partition are continued to August 10, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-09-2020 Continued To Oct-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Petition By Betty Williams
Off Calendar Aug-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Matthew Gluck appearing by CourtCall for Betty Williams (Respondent/Moving Party). John Boessenecker appearing by CourtCall for Shrita Williams (Petitioner/Opposing Party).

Matter called, argument is held on Respondent Betty Williams' Notice of Demurrer and Demurrer to First Amended Petition.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Respondent's request for judicial notice is granted under CCP section 452 subsections (d) and (h).

Demurrer to the First Cause of Action is overruled. A fair reading of this cause of action does not yield an allegation that Respondent and decedent entered into an oral agreement for sale or transfer of the real property in 1974.

Demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for declaratory relief is overruled.

Demurrer to the Third Cause of Action is sustained with leave to amend for Petitioners to plead sufficiently the elements of hostility to the co-ownership title, open claim by decedent to color of title or claim of right to the property, and ouster of the recorded co-owner (see Hacienda Ranch Homes, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2011) 198 Cal. App. 4th 1122, 1128-29, and Dimmick v. Dimmick (1962) 58 Cal. 2d 417, 422).

Demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action is sustained as to Respondent Ms. Williams without leave to amend, as she is not the trustee of the Williams Trust, and Petitioners have represented that are not bringing this cause of action against her.

The Demurrer to the Fifth Cause of Action is sustained as to Ms. Williams without leave to amend, as she is not the trustee of the Williams Trust, and Petitioners have represented that are not bringing this cause of action against her.

The Court ordered Mr. Gluck, as the prevailing party, to prepare a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and to submit it to the Court for review/signature.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-11-2020 Continued To Jun-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randall Boessenecker for Petitioners.
Matthew Gluck for and with Betty Williams (Respondent).

The Court noted that it does not yet have any opposition. Mr. Gluck stated that Ms. Williams was not properly served, that he has agreed to accept service and will file a substitution of attorney today, that he will file a response, an objection and a request for an evidentiary hearing, and that they have spoken and have agreed to a 90-day continuance. The Petition Concerning the Internal Affairs of a Trust, etc. is continued to June 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that it expects that an opposition be filed soon so that the Parties can know the shape of this.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition By Shrita Williams Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Declaratory Relief/Damages
Off Calendar Jan-29-2020 Continued To Mar-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randall Boessenecker for Shrita Williams and Gloria Williams.

Appearances: Randall Boessenecker for Shrita Williams and Gloria Williams.

The Court noted that Mr. Vann or anyone else are not present. Mr. Boessenecker stated that he has not heard from Mr. Vann, or received any response from him and that he did substituted service on Betty Williams. The Court raised the issue of Counsel providing legal authority in support of the causes of action, stated that it is not clear that the $3,500.00 payment made 44-years ago would constitute any relinquishment of rights, that it doesn't know what amounts were spent over time on the property, that it doesn't know if it is sufficient to cover the value of the property in an appropriate way and that it needs more legal and factual basis to show that the relief sought is appropriate. The Petition Concerning the Internal Affairs of a Trust, etc. is continued to March 11, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Counsel the opportunity to do so. Mr. Boessenecker is to get in touch with Mr. Vann to find out what he plans to do.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-22-2019 Continued To Jan-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randall Boessenecker for Petitioner.
Alan Seher for Lawrence Daniels (Trustee).
Dennis Vann specially appearing for and with Betty Williams.
Other family members also present.

Mr. Seher stated that his Client was dismissed from this action on August 20, 2019, that only the 4th and 5th causes of action applied to his Client, and that there is an outstanding issue of an improper lis pendens that he is hoping gets taken care of. Mr. Vann stated that his Client has not been properly served. Mr. Boessenecker stated that he will take care of service. The Petition Concerning the Internal Affairs of a Trust, etc. is continued to January 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition By Shrita Williams Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Declaratory Relief/Damages
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Continued To Jul-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Respondent Daniels Demurrer To Petition
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alan Scher, Esq. for Lawrence Daniels (Trustee).
Shirita Williams Ward, self-represented litigant, appeared by CourtCall.
Nicole Shaw was also present in the courtroom.

Matter called - Respondent Daniels' Demurrer to Petition.

Argument by Mr. Scher is held. At the conclusion of his argument, Mr. Scher inquired about the basis for the Court's ruling. The Court stated that the basis was set forth in the tentative ruling: no timely opposition was filed. Mr. Scher argued in support of a request to allow 10 days leave to amend in lieu of 30 days leave to amend stating that the Trust has been trying to sell the property. Ms. Williams stated that she had a major stroke and requested that the person on the line with her speak on her behalf. The Court stated that unless the person on CourtCall with Ms. Williams was an attorney that they could not speak on her behalf. Ms. Williams stated that she had paid Shaune Williams and that she is talking to several other lawyers now. The Court addressed Ms. Williams stating that this has been pending for some time and that it's been several months that Ms. Williams has known that she's needed an attorney. Mr. Scher stated that Ms. Williams could always proceed in pro per. Ms. Williams inquired if her Aunt could help her. The Court stated that her Aunt could not assist her unless she's an attorney. At the conclusion of discussions/argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: No timely opposition having been filed, the demurrer is sustained with 30 days leave to amend. The Court stated that if an amended petition is not filed with the 30 days and does not set forth a good faith basis that the petition could be dismissed. Mr. Scher requested that Ms. Williams waive notice. Ms. Williams inquired about waiving notice. The Court explained waiving notice stating that it meant that the 30 days would start from today. Ms. Williams waived notice. Mr. Scher requested that Ms. Williams provide contact information to himself and the Court. Ms. Williams stated that her mailing address is: 4726 Charade Drive, Houston, TX 77066. Ms. Williams provided the following phone number: 415.946.9402. Ms. Williams stated that Ms. Shaw was in court today and that she has power of attorney. The Court addressed Ms. Williams stating again for the record that only an attorney licensed in California could represent her in these proceedings. Mr. Scher is to prepare a form of order and is to include the language that any amended petition is to be filed by June 17, 2019. The Court stated that the petition should make allegations in good faith that meet the obejctions raised in the Demurrer.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter not reported.
